data.name: General_Language_Tooltip
data.value: Changes the language of GobchatEx's own settings window. Does not translate in-game chat text.

data.name: General_WindowTheme_Tooltip
data.value: Color scheme of this settings window. Dalamud Theme follows your Dalamud theme; the other themes bring their own colors and preview instantly.

data.name: General_OptionalPlugins_Tooltip
data.value: Plugins GobchatEx integrates with when they are available.

data.name: General_ChatTwo_NotInstalled
data.value: Chat 2 is not installed or not loaded.

data.name: General_ChatTwo_NoStyling
data.value: Chat 2 is loaded, but this version has no message styling support yet.

data.name: General_ShowQuickbar_Tooltip
data.value: Shows a small movable bar with quick toggles for GobchatEx's main features.

data.name: Quickbar_StartLog_NoFolder_Tooltip
data.value: Chat logging needs a log folder — choose one in the settings (Logs tab).

data.name: Quickbar_Hide_Tooltip
data.value: Hide the Quickbar. Re-enable it under General settings.

data.name: Quickbar_AttachToChat_Tooltip
data.value: Glue the Quickbar to the top edge of the chat window (Chat 2 when present, otherwise the game's chat log). The drag grip is hidden while attached; if no chat window is visible, the bar floats freely at its last position (or hides, while 'Hide when chat is hidden' is on).

data.name: Quickbar_HideDuringCutscenes_Tooltip
data.value: Hide the Quickbar during cutscenes and group pose.

data.name: Quickbar_HideWhenNotLoggedIn_Tooltip
data.value: Hide the Quickbar when you are not logged in to a character.

data.name: Quickbar_HideWhenUiHidden_Name
data.value: Hide when game UI is hidden

data.name: Quickbar_HideWhenUiHidden_Tooltip
data.value: Hide the Quickbar when the game UI is hidden.

data.name: Quickbar_HideInLoadingScreens_Tooltip
data.value: Hide the Quickbar during loading screens.

data.name: Quickbar_HideWhenChatHidden_Tooltip
data.value: Hide the Quickbar when no chat window is visible (Chat 2 or the game's chat log).

data.name: Formatting_ColorSelection_Tooltip
data.value: Each enabled type recolors matching chat text: Text color tints the letters, Text glow their outline. Right-click a swatch to clear it.

data.name: Formatting_DetectEmote_Tooltip
data.value: When direct speech is marked, everything else will be automatically flagged as emote.

data.name: Mentions_Highlight_SuppressSelf
data.value: Don't highlight mentions for my own messages

data.name: Mentions_Highlight_SuppressSelf_Tooltip
data.value: Messages you send (including outgoing tells) keep their Say/Emote/OOC styling but skip the mention highlight. /echo is exempt so you can always test your mentions there. Already-printed lines don't change — send a new message after toggling.

data.name: Mentions_Sound_PlayOnMatch
data.value: Play a sound when a mention matches

data.name: Mentions_Sound_PlayOnMatch_Tooltip
data.value: Game sound effects follow the game's sound-effects volume; a custom file has its own volume slider.

data.name: Mentions_Sound_SuppressSelf
data.value: Don't play for my own messages

data.name: Mentions_Global_Header_Tooltip
data.value: Words and names that count as mentions (case-insensitive whole words).

data.name: Mentions_Trigger_Remove_Tooltip
data.value: Hold Ctrl+Shift and click to remove this word.

data.name: Mentions_Player_Header_Tooltip
data.value: Match your character's name automatically. Add your character with the button below and keep it active.

data.name: Mentions_Player_Empty
data.value: No characters added yet. Add the current character above.

data.name: Mentions_Player_CurrentNotActive
data.value: Mentions of {0} won't match — add this character and keep it active.

data.name: Mentions_Character_Remove_Tooltip
data.value: Hold Ctrl+Shift and click to remove this character.

data.name: Mentions_Character_Miqote_Tooltip
data.value: Also matches the short form of an apostrophe name, e.g. A'nabelle → nabelle. Only available when this character's name contains an apostrophe.

data.name: Mentions_Character_Partial_Tooltip
data.value: Matches as a substring anywhere inside a word, not just as a whole word. Requires the matching name part on the left to be enabled.

data.name: Mentions_Character_Fuzzy_Tooltip
data.value: Also matches close misspellings of the names above, e.g. "Elora" for "Elara".

data.name: Mentions_Character_NameStyle_Tooltip
data.value: Color and glow for every match derived from this character's name above (full name, first/last name, partial, Miqo'te, fuzzy). Right-click a swatch to clear it back to the default mention color.

data.name: Mentions_CustomWord_Remove_Tooltip
data.value: Hold Ctrl+Shift and click to remove this word.

data.name: Mentions_Test_Header_Tooltip
data.value: Type a sample message to see what would count as a mention right now — trigger words, character names and the fuzzy level apply exactly as they do in chat.

data.name: Groups_Custom_Header_Tooltip
data.value: Sort players into named, colored groups. A player in more than one group shows the earlier group's color.

data.name: Groups_Custom_Remove_Tooltip
data.value: Hold Ctrl+Shift and click to remove this group.

data.name: Groups_Custom_DuplicateName_Error
data.value: A group with this name already exists.

data.name: Groups_Custom_Player_Remove_Tooltip
data.value: Hold Ctrl+Shift and click to remove this player.

data.name: Groups_Custom_NumericName_Error
data.value: A group name can't be just a number (that would be ambiguous with the /gobchat group command's index).

data.name: Groups_Custom_AddTarget_Tooltip
data.value: Adds your current target, {0}, to this group.

data.name: Groups_Custom_NoPlayerTarget
data.value: Target a player first (NPCs, monsters, and other non-player targets can't be added).

data.name: Groups_Friend_Header_Tooltip
data.value: The game's own seven friend-list groups. Assign players to these from the Friend List window in-game.

data.name: Groups_ChatTwoBackground_Tooltip
data.value: Message background color in Chat 2. Right-click to clear.

data.name: About_Description
data.value: GobchatEx highlights roleplay in the native chat log: quoted speech, emotes, OOC and mention trigger words each get their own colors.

data.name: About_Lineage
data.value: Based on the idea of Gobchat by MarbleBag and GobchatEx by Shuro - reimagined as a Dalamud plugin instead of an external tool.

data.name: About_ArtCredit
data.value: Character art and logo illustrated by TsuShi Illustrations.

data.name: Changelog_V1_0_0_ChatHighlighting
data.value: Say, Emote, and OOC dialogue color in the native chat log.

data.name: Changelog_V1_0_0_PlayerMentions
data.value: Player Mentions match your character's actual name - full, first, last, partial - with fuzzy typo matching.

data.name: Changelog_V1_0_0_PlayerGroups
data.value: Player Groups: color-code friends and RP partners, plus the game's 7 friend-list symbol groups.

data.name: Changelog_V1_0_0_RangeFilter
data.value: Range Filter: fade or hide chat from far-away players.

data.name: Changelog_V1_0_0_MentionSounds
data.value: Mention alerts: game sound effect or custom audio file (wav/mp3/ogg), with cooldown.

data.name: Changelog_V1_0_0_GroupSounds
data.value: Per-group alert sounds: configurable per custom group.

data.name: Changelog_V1_0_0_MentionHistory
data.value: Mention History: review older mentions of trigger words.

data.name: Changelog_V1_0_0_ChatCommands
data.value: Chat commands for macros: run /gex help for the full list.

data.name: ChatLog_Button_StartStop_Tooltip
data.value: Logging never starts by itself and always stops at logout. Stopping does not finalize the current file — starting again during the same login continues it; a new file begins at login, character switch, or when the folder settings change.

data.name: ChatLog_Button_NoFolder_Tooltip
data.value: Logging needs a log folder — choose one below.

data.name: ChatLog_Status_NoFolder
data.value: Choose a log folder below to enable logging.

data.name: ChatLog_Status_WaitingForMessage
data.value: Logging is active — the log file is created with the first message.

data.name: ChatLog_Folder_Tooltip
data.value: Log files are written here, one file per login or character switch. There is no default folder — logging stays disabled until one is chosen.

data.name: ChatLog_Folder_Reset_Tooltip
data.value: Clear the folder. Logging is disabled until a new one is chosen.

data.name: ChatLog_Folder_InvalidPath
data.value: The configured folder is not usable — logging is disabled until it is fixed.

data.name: ChatLog_CharacterFolders_Tooltip
data.value: Each character's log files go into their own subfolder, named after the character.

data.name: ChatLog_Channels_Tooltip
data.value: Only messages from the selected channels are written to the log file.

data.name: Formatting_Reset_Tooltip
data.value: Hold Ctrl+Shift and click to reset to the default color.

data.name: Formatting_ImportGame_Tooltip
data.value: Import the game's own color for this channel (Character Configuration → Log Text Color), mapped to the closest UIColor row.

data.name: Range_FadeOut_Tooltip
data.value: Up to this distance, messages stay fully readable; beyond it they darken step by step.

data.name: Range_CutOff_Tooltip
data.value: Beyond this distance messages dim to the darkest step in the native log — never removed, so they stay in the chat history and logs. In Chat 2 they can be hidden from display only. Set it equal to the fade start for a hard transition without fading. The slider goes up to 60 yalms; Ctrl+click it to type a higher value.

data.name: Range_Preview_Tooltip
data.value: Shows both distances as rings around your character for about 8 seconds: yellow = fade start, orange = cut-off.

data.name: Range_MentionsIgnore_Tooltip
data.value: A message that mentions you is never faded or hidden, no matter how far away the sender is.

data.name: Range_Channels_Tooltip
data.value: Only proximity channels are offered — filtering a server-wide channel by distance would hide messages based on where the sender happens to be standing.

data.name: Range_EngineLimit_Tooltip
data.value: The game engine already limits this channel to a maximum of 20 yalms — messages from further away never arrive, regardless of the distances set above.

data.name: Range_ChatTwo_Header_Tooltip
data.value: In Chat 2 the range filter can use real per-message transparency instead of the darkened color steps of the native chat log.

data.name: Range_StartOpacity_Tooltip
data.value: Initial opacity at fade out distance.

data.name: Range_EndOpacity_Tooltip
data.value: Final opacity at cut off distance, before it disappears completely.

data.name: ChatTwo_Status_NotConnected
data.value: Not connected to Chat 2's message styling.

data.name: ChatTwo_NotConnected_Hint
data.value: Requires the Chat 2 plugin with message styling support.

data.name: ChatTwo_NotConnected_Tooltip
data.value: Needs a connection to Chat 2's message styling.

data.name: ChatTwo_Tabs_Header_Tooltip
data.value: Choose which styling may apply in each Chat 2 tab. Unchecked features are suppressed there — for example, never hide anything in a tab that should log everything.

data.name: ChatTwo_Tabs_Empty
data.value: No tabs known yet — Chat 2 sends its tab list once connected.

data.name: ColorPicker_NoRecolor_Tooltip
data.value: No recolor. Click to choose a color.

data.name: Sound_FileMissing
data.value: File not found — the game sound effect plays instead.

data.name: Sound_PreviewFailed
data.value: The file could not be played — the game sound effect plays instead (details in the plugin log).

data.name: Sound_FileTooLong
data.value: The file is {0:0.#} seconds long — alert sounds work best at {1:0} seconds or less.

data.name: Groups_Sound_PlayOnMessage
data.value: Play a sound when a group member's message arrives

data.name: Groups_Sound_PlayOnMessage_Tooltip
data.value: At most one sound plays per message — when the message also matches a mention and the mention sound is enabled, the mention sound wins. Your own messages never play a group sound.

data.name: Groups_Sound_Cooldown_Tooltip
data.value: Shared by all groups: after any group sound plays, further group sounds stay quiet for this long.

data.name: MentionHistory_Sender_Tooltip
data.value: Right-click to add or remove this player from a group.

data.name: Commands_Primary_Help
data.value: Open the GobchatEx settings window. Type /gex help for the full command list.

data.name: Commands_Alias_Help
data.value: Alias for /gex — open the GobchatEx settings window.

data.name: Commands_Group_InvalidSyntax
data.value: Invalid syntax. Try: /gex group <name or number> add|remove|clear <player> [world]

data.name: Commands_Mention_InvalidSyntax
data.value: Invalid syntax. Try: /gex mention add|remove <word> or /gex mention list

data.name: Commands_Log_NoFolder
data.value: No log folder chosen — pick one in the settings' Logs tab first.

data.name: Commands_Log_StatusOn
data.value: Chat logging is on — the log file is created with the first message.

data.name: Commands_Log_StatusOnFile
data.value: Chat logging is on — writing to {0}.

data.name: Commands_Help_ConfigOpen
data.value: /gex config open - open the settings window.

data.name: Commands_Help_Group
data.value: /gex group <number or name> add|remove|clear <player> [world] - edit a custom group's player list.

data.name: Commands_Help_GroupList
data.value: /gex group list - list your custom groups with their numbers.

data.name: Commands_Help_PlayerCount
data.value: /gex player count - how many players are nearby.

data.name: Commands_Help_PlayerList
data.value: /gex player list - nearby players with their distance.

data.name: Commands_Help_PlayerDistance
data.value: /gex player distance <name> - your distance to one player.

data.name: Commands_Help_Mention
data.value: /gex mention add|remove <word> - add or remove a mention trigger word.

data.name: Commands_Help_MentionList
data.value: /gex mention list - list your mention trigger words.

data.name: Commands_Help_Log
data.value: /gex log start|stop|status - control chat logging for this session.

data.name: General_LegacyEchoFallback_Tooltip
data.value: When enabled, GobchatEx also recognizes old-style "/e gc ..." echo commands from the original desktop app, so existing macros keep working. Only your own local echo messages are read — nothing is sent to other players. Note: any echo text that happens to start with "gc " (yours or another plugin's) may trigger a harmless "Unknown command" reply; disable this if that becomes annoying.
